projectDescription: QueryComment is a database observability platform that correlates query performance, engine internals, and infrastructure metrics across three layers to detect and explain database degradation before it impacts users.
keyFeatures: Three-layer database observability: query performance, engine internals, and infrastructure metrics
Cross-layer causal correlation that explains why a query got slow, not just that it did
Drift detection using statistical trend analysis to catch silent degradation before thresholds are breached
Query fingerprinting with per-fingerprint latency percentile baselines (p50, p95, p99)
PostgreSQL and MySQL support across self-managed and managed platforms (Supabase, Neon, Aurora, PlanetScale, RDS, Cloud SQL)
No code changes or query proxying required — agent reads existing database statistics views
Autovacuum lag detection, bloat monitoring, and lock contention analysis for PostgreSQL
InnoDB buffer pool, replication health, and metadata lock monitoring for MySQL
Cloud-specific infrastructure signals (EBS burst credits, GCP persistent disk limits, live migration events)
publicApiSurface: Early access signup / Calendly onboarding flow
Dashboard with query regression alerts and causal chain explanations
